updated_conclusion: The full 947 win64c provider descriptor population is now clustered by native setup-function ownership. A full .rdata provider scan returned all 378 candidate descriptors, attached address references, classified setup versus clone/materializer refs, and grouped the descriptors into 99 setup-function clusters.

tooling_milestone.cluster_rule: A reference with .pdata function ownership and not matching a clone/materializer thunk address is treated as a setup reference; clone/materializer thunk references are kept as table mechanics.

known_login_settings_cluster_locations.family: shared directlogin-adjacent plus vt-varc/source-provider setup function
console provider cluster with one additional sibling

new_observations: Every discovered provider descriptor has at least one setup reference after full-scan ref enrichment, so the 378-row population can be navigated by 99 function-level work items.
The largest setup functions publish broad descriptor families: the top five clusters cover 146 descriptors total.
The known login/settings descriptors are no longer isolated address facts; they sit inside setup-function clusters that expose nearby sibling descriptors for follow-up.
The shared 0x1400CCA20 setup function contains three descriptors in the full clustered scan, including the previously labeled vt-varc/source-provider-adjacent and directlogin-adjacent rows.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-scan-rdata: Promotes the 378 raw descriptor candidates into function-grouped work items.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-address-ref-func: Applies function attribution to the entire provider population, not just five known rows.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-descriptor-nativ: Places the known settings provider families in their wider setup-function neighborhoods.

bounded_inference.statement: Setup-function clusters are now the preferred navigation layer for this provider descriptor family. They identify which functions publish groups of provider adapters and which nearby descriptors should be investigated together.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for clustering mechanics, medium for subsystem naming until each cluster's setup function is traced

next_targets: Add compact CSV/JSON export for setup-function clusters so the 99-cluster worklist is easy to triage without loading the full 3 MB raw artifact.
Trace the largest cluster 0x1400E3AF0-0x1400E4DE4 to identify what subsystem owns its 41 descriptors.
Trace the shared known cluster 0x1400CCA20-0x1400CCFFC to resolve the directlogin-adjacent versus vt-varc/source-provider relationship.
Add string/literal enrichment to setup-function clusters so cluster labels can be inferred from nearby static strings.
